content:
# Recommendations ‚Äî next plan iter (post iter-023)

## HIGH ‚Äî closest to completion: B6 succ `tensorPowAdd_assoc` via the VALIDATED hom-level route
The refactor leaves B6 succ (@3230) as a **braiding-free pentagon** whose only obstruction is the
dependently-typed `eqToIso(add_assoc m m' (c+1))` blocking the *iso-level* `Iso.trans_assoc`. The unblock
was **proven this iter on the sibling `tensorPowAdd_zero_left` succ**. Dispatch ONE prover lane executing it:
1. `apply Iso.ext` FIRST (drop to hom level) ‚Äî at hom level `eqToIso‚ÜíeqToHom` and the `Œ±'' .hom ‚â´ Œ±''.inv`
   pair cancels by `Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc` (no dependent-`‚â™‚â´` motive).
2. Canonical `simp only` bridge (`Iso.trans_hom/symm_hom`, `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso_eq`,
   `tensorObjWhiskerLeftIso_eq`, `tensorObjAssoc`, `whiskerRightIso_hom/inv`, `whiskerLeftIso_hom/inv`,
   `Category.assoc`, `Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc`, `hom_inv_whiskerRight_assoc`, `whiskerLeft_hom_inv_assoc`,
   `Iso.cancel_iso_inv_left`).
3. Fold `ih`/`ihR` by `rw`; `congr 1` peel shared prefixes; discharge the `(m+m')+c = m+(m'+c)` reindexer
   with a `tensorObjIso_succ_reindex`-style `subst` helper; close the residual pentagon with `monoidal`.
- **Pre-committed reverse signal:** if a `tensorBraiding` reappears in the residual the refactor is wrong
  (analogist verified zero braidings ‚Äî very unlikely). If instead `monoidal` resists the pentagon, do NOT
  retry the monolith: **effort-break the `(m+m')+c` reindexer into a named `subst` lemma** (analogue of
  `tensorObjIso_succ_reindex`) then `monoidal`. Concrete, not a re-run of a failed approach.
- Once B6 closes, **B7 `sectionsMul_mul_assoc` (@3260) unblocks immediately** ‚Äî assemble exactly like
  `sectionsMul_mul_one` (ingredients (1)=B5 + (3)=B6 are named; only the (2) section-slide remains).

## MEDIUM ‚Äî .lean cleanup (lean-auditor iter023, 3 major + 1 minor; I cannot edit .lean)
Direct a `refactor`/prover lane to delete stale guidance that will mislead the next prover:
- **`canonical_runit_core` @2964 is DEAD** (sole call site eliminated by the refactor; `mul_one` now closes
  by `rfl`-chain). Remove the `@[reassoc] private lemma` + its docstring. NOTE: I gave its blueprint proof
  block (@1116) a restored `\leanok` (it is still proven/axiom-clean) ‚Äî if the decl is removed, drop that
  block too.
- **Stale planner-strategy block @2077‚Äì2138** and **`tensorPowAdd` docstring @2142‚Äì2146** describe the OLD
  1st-index/braiding recursion ‚Äî directly contradict the code. Rewrite to the 2nd-index braiding-free story.
- **Minor:** stale comment "single remaining gap of `sectionsMul_mul_one`" @3082 ‚Äî proof is closed.

## MEDIUM ‚Äî 1-to-1 coverage debt (new this iter; lvbc flagged unpinned)
Blueprint these `private` helpers (they are load-bearing for B6 ‚Äî currently invisible to the DAG):
- `tensorObjIso_succ_reindex` (`SectionGradedRing.lean` @2987) ‚Äî `subst h; simp`; reindexes
  `(tensorObjIso (L^a) L).inv ‚â´ (eqToHom ‚ñ∑ L) ‚â´ (tensorObjIso (L^b) L).hom` to a single `eqToHom`.
- `tensorObjWhiskerRightIso_tensorObj` (@3121) ‚Äî hand-built `whiskerRight_tensor`:
  `e ‚ñ∑ (A‚äóB) = Œ±‚Åª¬π ‚â™‚â´ ((e‚ñ∑A)‚ñ∑B) ‚â™‚â´ Œ±`; proof = `Iso.ext` + `‚Üê whiskerRight_tensor` + `‚Üê whisker_exchange`.
- (`archon dag-query unmatched` = 342 nodes total, but the rest are inherited GMul/GOne/Grassmannian/
  GradedModule merge-back artifacts, unchanged across iters 011‚Äì023 ‚Äî defer to merge-back.)

## DO NOT RE-ASSIGN
- **3 commutativity lemmas** (`tensorBraiding_self_eq_id_of_isInvertible`, `tensorPowAdd_comm`,
  `sectionsMul_mul_comm`) ‚Äî `sectionsMul_mul_comm` is FALSE for general `L`; only `[IsInvertible L]`; no
  consumer; FUTURE.
- **4 œá sorries** (`QuotScheme.lean`) ‚Äî œá-semantic, need the cohomology leg; DEFERRED.

## Trajectory note for the planner (must read before assigning B6)
Sorry has been flat at 5 for **7 iters** (017‚Üí023). iter-023 is a *genuine structural reset*, not churn:
braiding eliminated, `tensorPowAdd_zero_left` + both unit laws newly closed, base case closed, the
018‚Äì022 braided tower retired, and the residual is now a single braiding-free pentagon with a hom-level
closure route validated on a sibling. The iter-022 escalate-to-USER gate was overridden by Standing
directive #1 (refactor-out-of-dead-ends) ‚Äî defensible, since the refactor dissolved the actual obstruction
class. **But the hom-level route is validated on the EASIER sibling, not yet on the harder pentagon.** If
the next prover lane does NOT close B6 succ, that is the signal the "validated route" does not transfer ‚Äî
at that point re-surface to the user (per the standing gate's intent) rather than a third grind.

## DURABLE INFRA ‚Äî `sync_leanok` over-strip (10th consecutive iter; build-breaking)
`sync_leanok` again blanket-stripped proof-block `\leanok` from `Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex` (removed 24)
AND emitted a build-breaking `\(`/`\)` corruption (@1543, plastex `RecursionError`). Review repairs it every
iter; the fix is **sync-side and now urgent**: (a) the strip regex must not eat math delimiters / proof
prose, only the `\leanok` token; (b) skip re-stripping decls that `lean_verify` axiom-clean on this large
file (the heartbeat glitch mis-reads them as failing). Surfaced in `TO_USER.md`.

## Out-of-cone (merge-back, unchanged)
Blueprint-doctor: broken `\ref`/`\cref`/`\uses` in `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` (covers two non-existent
`.lean` files), `Picard_GlueDescent.tex`, `Picard_QuotScheme.tex` ‚Äî all outside the 3-seed cone; resolve at
merge-back, do NOT edit.
